name: Easy Day Vegetable Lasagna
recipeIngredient: 48 ounces Jar low-fat spaghetti sauce (any flavor)
10 ounces Frozen chopped spinach (defrosted and drained)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Spray a 9 x 13-inch baking pan with non-stick vegetable coating.
3. Saute green onions and mushrooms in soy oil until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
4. Add spaghetti sauce and simmer for 5 minutes.
5. Set aside.
6. In a bowl, combine tofu, spinach, egg, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and oregano. Mix well.
7. In the baking dish, begin with a layer of the spaghetti sauce mixture.
8. Add a layer of uncooked lasagna noodles.
9. Spread the tofu mixture evenly over the noodles.
10. Continue with more layers of noodles and sauce until the dish is complete.
11. Top with remaining sauce and sprinkle cheese on top.
12. Cover with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes.
13. Remove foil and continue baking for an additional 15 minutes, or until heated through and bubbly.
14. Let stand for 10 minutes before serving. Serves 6-8.
Option: Layer one 16-ounce package of frozen vegetable medley between layers of lasagna noodles or saute 1/2 cup fresh vegetable combination to add extra flavor and nutrition.
